description:
You are a biophysicist preparing a figure for a review article on ion channel selectivity mechanisms.

PDB:1K4C is the high-resolution (2.0 Å) crystal structure of the KcsA potassium channel from Streptomyces lividans (Zhou et al., Nature 2001). This tetrameric channel (chains A, B, C, D) contains the selectivity filter — a narrow pore lined by the signature sequence TVGYG (Thr75–Val76–Gly77–Tyr78–Gly79) from each subunit. Dehydrated K⁺ ions are coordinated by backbone carbonyl oxygens within the filter.

The PDB file is available at: /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/structures/1K4C.pdb

Your tasks:
1. Load 1K4C into PyMOL
2. Identify the tetrameric channel architecture
3. Locate the selectivity filter (TVGYG motif, residues 75–79)
4. Identify K⁺ ions bound within the selectivity filter
5. Measure the pore diameter at the selectivity filter — specifically, the distance between opposing backbone carbonyl oxygens across the pore (e.g., between chain A and chain C at a filter residue)
6. Create a publication-quality figure showing the pore with the selectivity filter residues displayed as sticks, K⁺ ions as spheres, and the protein backbone as cartoon. Orient the view to show a cross-section or side view of the pore.
7. Save the figure as: /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/kcsa_selectivity_filter.png
8. Write a quantitative report to: /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/kcsa_filter_report.txt

The report must include:
- The number of K⁺ ions identified in/near the selectivity filter
- At least one distance measurement (in Ångströms) between opposing carbonyl oxygens across the pore
- Identification of the selectivity filter residues (by residue number and/or the TVGYG sequence)

Both the PNG figure and the text report are required outputs.
